items.name: Fixed Work Hours
items.description: Set specific start and end times and stick to them consistently
items.best_for: Remote workers who struggle with boundary blurring
items.pros: Clear boundary, predictable schedule, enables planning
items.cons: Requires discipline, may conflict with team expectations
items.implementation: Block calendar 9 AM-5:30 PM, set Slack status after hours

items.name: Shutdown Ritual
items.description: A consistent end-of-work routine that signals mental disconnection
items.best_for: Developers who can't 'turn off' after work
items.pros: Clear mental boundary, reduces evening anxiety, improves sleep
items.cons: Takes 10 min daily, requires consistency to work
items.implementation: Review tasks, write tomorrow's plan, say 'shutdown complete'

items.name: Technology Boundaries
items.description: Separate work and personal devices/accounts, use Do Not Disturb
items.best_for: People who check Slack/email compulsively after hours
items.cons: May miss genuine emergencies, requires on-call policy
items.implementation: Separate work phone, DND after 6 PM, no work email on personal device

items.name: Energy Management
items.description: Match high-energy tasks to peak hours, protect low-energy time
items.best_for: People who feel exhausted despite working 'normal' hours
items.pros: Works with your biology, not against it, sustainable
items.cons: Requires self-awareness, may conflict with team schedule
items.implementation: Deep work 9-11 AM, meetings 1-4 PM, admin 4-5 PM

items.name: Social Boundaries
items.description: Protect non-work relationships and activities as fiercely as work commitments
items.best_for: People whose social life has deteriorated due to work
items.pros: Prevents isolation, improves wellbeing, provides perspective
items.cons: May require saying no to work events, can feel uncomfortable
items.implementation: Weekly dinner with friends, no work at family events, hobby time

comparisons.insight: The most effective approach combines multiple strategies: fixed hours + shutdown ritual + technology boundaries. Start with one, build consistency, then add others. The key isn't which strategy you pick — it's that you communicate and enforce it.

keyInsight: Work-life balance requires ACTIVE strategies, not passive hope. Combine fixed work hours, shutdown rituals, and technology boundaries. Communicate them clearly and enforce them consistently. The first 2 weeks are hardest — after that, people respect your boundaries.
